body:
## Summary
- Wire Settings to `useAccessibilityStore.persistenceWarning` and render accessibility-scoped warning copy for theme, text, and audio preference storage failures.
- Preserve study-data warning behavior for progress/mistake stores and surface corrupt progress JSON reads as recoverable warnings again.
- Loosen validator source checks so Prettier's compact Settings import and the read-warning progress hydration shape remain accepted.

## Validation
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" npm run validate:content`
- `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false`
- `NODE_OPTIONS="--v8-pool-size=1" node --test tests/content-storage-write-fail-soft.test.js tests/content-settings-route-copy-parity.test.js --test-name-pattern "PersistenceWarningNotice|local study data|persistence warning|settings route shell copy"`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`

Note: the full queue-provided broad `tests/v1-1-accessibility.test.js` pattern still trips the pre-existing app-wide dark-theme migration guard for `app/(tabs)/practice.tsx`, which is outside this PR's scope.
title: [codex] settings: scope accessibility persistence warnings
